purpose: Close the remaining Result.kt permission false-positive in the bounded phase-02 data/repository group.

set.sourceBehavior: `Result.kt` only defines the repository `Results` sealed class and `successOr` helper; it does not request permissions or touch protected APIs.
set.targetBehavior: Phase-02 repository translation does not need a target analogue for permission handling here because the source result wrapper is permission-free and repository permission rows are already closed on the actual repository files.
verificationEvidence: Manual source review confirmed `Result.kt` contains only the `Results.Success`, `Results.Error`, and `successOr` helper declarations.
The phase-02 repository and login/session target modules contain no result-wrapper permission path, and `entry/src/main/module.json5::requestPermissions` remains empty for this phase.

A bounded `cjpm test --target aarch64-linux-ohos` reproduction from `entry/src/test/cangjie` now gets past the original `phase02_repository_contract_test.cj` lambda syntax blocker and then fails later in `phase01_resource_theme_contract_test.cj` on `ResourceStr` versus `String` `@Assert` generic inference.
set.notes: Static-analysis false positive: the source result wrapper is data-only and has no permission behavior.

testEvidence.detail: `cjpm test --target aarch64-linux-ohos` from `entry/src/test/cangjie` now gets past the original phase-02 repository lambda syntax error and then fails later in `phase01_resource_theme_contract_test.cj` because `std.unittest @Assert` cannot infer `ResourceStr` route-label assertions.

manualEvidence.detail: `Result.kt` is a data-only sealed result wrapper with no permission or protected API behavior.
